design_philosophy.core_loop: Cycle through waves of enemies using directional attacks, special moves, and weapons; progress through urban environments toward final boss confrontation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing health resource via Special Attacks (health-cost offensive option) while handling increasing enemy density and variety across stages
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Street-level vigilante justice: rescue kidnapped ally by fighting through criminal underworld with cooperative partner
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberately balanced character asymmetry (Max is slow/powerful, Skate is fast/weak) creating distinct playstyles; combat mechanics directly influenced by Street Fighter II's depth rather than mimicking button-mashing conventions of early beat-em-ups

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple continues available; progression allows retry from last stage
difficulty.continues_system: Continue system present, enabling extended play sessions
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; players continue from beginning of last stage upon losing lives
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection provides meaningful difficulty variance (Max/Blaze vs Axel/Skate trade speed for power); two-player cooperative play reduces individual execution pressure

progression.upgrade_system: Weapons and consumable items dropped mid-stage by defeated enemies; health recovery items scattered throughout levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Progression through stages is mandatory sequence; Duel mode available as secondary competitive content
replayability_hooks: Four distinct playable characters with unique movesets and speed/power tradeoffs
Duel mode for competitive play between players
Different enemy placement and difficulty scaling based on character selection

world_building_techniques: Story framing: opening scene establishes prior victory and hero retreat from city
Inciting incident delivered via phone call (Skate reports Adam's kidnapping)
Visual narrative: protagonist photo attached to door as symbolic threat
Location progression from urban Wood Oak City to Mr. X's isolated island hideout conveys escalation
Single canonical ending (unlike predecessor): Mr. X defeated, Adam rescued, escape via helicopter

boss_design.philosophy: Boss encounters represent narrative antagonists requiring pattern recognition and execution; climactic confrontation with Mr. X and his bodyguard Shiva serves as final gauntlet
notable_bosses: Shiva (Mr. X's bodyguard, second phase or final encounter)

faction_indicators: Police force (Adam Hunter's affiliation, represented as legitimate authority)
Vigilante group (player characters operating outside law)
danger_signals: Kidnapping as coercive tactic and revenge mechanism
Brazen daytime criminal activity: 'beatings and looting took place regularly and in broad daylight'
Escalation: crime worse than previous game despite prior victory
Symbolic threat: photo of victim chained at antagonist's feet
environmental_storytelling: Adam's house left 'in ruin' establishing crime wave severity
Urban Wood Oak City locations as contested territory
Desolate island hideout as criminal stronghold geography
Progression from city streets to isolated fortress reflects escalating stakes
